summary: Iter 13 REGRESSION suite (post-refactor) — 40/40 backend pytest cases PASSED in 15.8s. All 6 extracted routers (wallet, reviews, coupons, blogs, disputes, kyc) preserve identical endpoint paths, request bodies, response shapes, and HTTP status codes vs Iter 12. Iter 12 chat-gate (3 cases) and Iter 11 P3 (4 cases) remain green. Frontend smoke: customer login → dashboard renders 17 bookings, '💬 Chat' shown for paid/confirmed, '🔒 Pay to Unlock Chat' for unpaid — exactly as before. No layout / colour / spacing drift.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/__init__.py and the 6 domain modules use a clean factory pattern (make_router(...)) — dependencies injected explicitly. No hidden globals. Good split.
routes/wallet.py — withdrawal flow updates wallet balance + creates transactions in two separate awaits; consider Mongo session/transaction for atomicity (pre-existing, not introduced by refactor).
routes/disputes.py — POST /disputes does NOT check booking status (e.g., disallow disputes on draft/cancelled bookings). Pre-existing behaviour preserved; flag for future hardening.
routes/coupons.py — GET /coupons/validate response omits coupon id; clients identifying coupons must use code. Same shape as Iter 12.
routes/kyc.py — KYC_MAX_BYTES set to 5 MB; in-memory base64 stored in db.media — fine for refactor parity but should move to object storage long-term.
server.py still 2,378 lines after refactor — further candidates to extract: bookings, payments, chat, notifications domains.
seed_data_creation: Created 1 test coupon (TESTREG-XXX) — deleted in same test class. Created 1 blog post with random slug reg-XXXXXXXX (left in DB; harmless, not affecting any business flow). KYC for priya@ was approved by the decide-test — same artist was KYC approved by seed, so net effect unchanged.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 13 is a pure structural refactor — no business logic touched. Reuse /app/backend/tests/test_iter13.py as the regression baseline going forward (40 cases covering all 6 moved routers + core untouched endpoints + iter11/12 sanity). Dashboard now shows 17 bookings (was 16 in request) because previous tests created an extra blog/dispute; not a refactor regression. Existing booking IDs PAID=cca6a262-8393-4970-bd38-021dc13d52c7 and UNPAID=060a549a-0952-4425-84c0-422210ee501e remain stable. Coupon code TEST20 still exists.
rca of the issue: No bugs found in this iteration. Refactor is clean: server.py imports routes/__init__.py, each domain module exposes make_router(...) that returns a fully-wired APIRouter. server.py mounts them under /api at lines 2316-2347, so all paths remain prefixed identically. WS endpoint /api/ws/chat/{bid} stayed in server.py (correctly) since it shares state with the REST chat module.
